exemplar.title: To what extent do 10Î¼L (Â±0.2) samples of 5 different plant-based protein sources (Phaseolus vulgaris L (kidney bean), Arachis hypogaea (peanuts), Triticum aestivum (wheat flour), Vigna radiata (green grams), and Persea americana (avocado)) vary in branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) concentration, measured by thin layer chromatography (TLC) and analysed using the Hue, Saturation, Lightness (HSL) colour model?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do 10Î¼L (Â±0.2) samples of 5 different plant-based protein sources (Phaseolus vulgaris L (kidney bean), Arachis hypogaea (peanuts), Triticum aestivum (wheat flour), Vigna radiata (green grams), and Persea americana (avocado)) vary in branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) concentration, measured by thin layer chromatography (TLC) and analysed using the Hue, Saturation, Lightness (HSL) colour model?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question framed in a specific, relevant context of vegan athletesâ BCAA needs
Detailed methodological rationale for variable control, solvent choice, detection method and safety protocols
Step-by-step procedure given with quantities, timings and equipment to allow reproducibility
Comprehensive background linking chemical details of BCAAs to the investigationâs objectives
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities in solvent mixture composition (ethanolâbutanol ratio) and camera settings
Drop volume assumption without calibrationâmicropipette drop size not justified
Uncertainty value (Â±0.2 ÂµL) stated without source or calibration data
Alternative hypothesis wording and table headings lack clear units
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores are 6, 5 and 5. The research context is exemplary (band 5â6), while methodological explanations and procedure description have minor omissions that prevent full depth (band 5). Holistically this aligns with a score of 5 for Research Design.

strengths: Raw data well organised in labelled tables; processed data graph with error bars communicates results clearly
Instrument uncertainties identified and percentage uncertainties calculated
Appropriate selection and execution of means, standard deviations and one-way ANOVA for the research question
weaknesses: Some table headings misaligned and missing units reducing precision
Uncertainties were not propagated into mean values or ANOVA
Reliability test (SD <33 % rule) is conceptually weak
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores are 4 (communication), 3 (uncertainties) and 4 (processing quality). Overall the analysis is clear but with significant omissions and inaccuracies, placing it firmly in the 3â4 band, so a holistic score of 4 is justified.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question and is fully consistent with the processed data and ANOVA results
Interpretation of inverse L-values as concentration measures is correct
Relevant comparison to peer-reviewed studies and proteinâcontent literature supports the findings
weaknesses: P-value reported as 0 rather than a conventionally formatted value (e.g. p < 0.001)
Mechanistic biochemical explanations for the observed correlations are only briefly asserted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands meet the criteria for band 5: the conclusion is relevant, consistent and justified, and comparisons to scientific context are integrated. Depth is strong but not exhaustive, so a score of 5 is warranted.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses and their impacts are identified and explained
Proposed improvements are realistic, relevant to the identified issues, and explained in terms of dataâquality benefits
Strengths in data reliability (e.g. outlier handling) are clearly discussed
weaknesses: Discussion of uncertainty impact is brief and lacks quantification of its influence on conclusions
Cost and feasibility of suggested improvements are not addressed
Random error table (Table 11) is empty and needs completion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand demonstrates specific identification and explanation of weaknesses (band 5) and realistic, relevant improvements with explained effects (band 5). The evaluation is thorough but could be deeper in feasibility and quantification, supporting a holistic score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly answers the research question, interprets mean L-values correctly (inverse to concentration) and is fully supported by the processed data and ANOVA result.  Minor slips in wording about SD outliers do not undermine consistency, so a strong score in the top band is justified.

Raw data are organised in clearly labelled tables; processed data and a bar graph include error bars.  Small inconsistencies (misaligned headings, spelling, missing units for some L values) reduce precision, yet overall communication is clearly understandable.  This matches the descriptor of being either clear OR precise rather than fully both.

Means, standard deviations and a one-way ANOVA are appropriate for the question and mostly calculated correctly, though an SD example contains rounding inconsistencies and the reliability test (33 % of mean rule) is conceptually weak.  Processing is therefore adequate with some inaccuracies, aligning with the 3-4 band.

Specific methodological weaknesses (lighting variation, manual Rf measurement, solvent polarity) are identified, and their differential impact on reliability or accuracy is explicitly explained in Table 10.  The causal links demonstrate understanding, meriting placement in the highest band (score 5 rather than 6 due to brief discussion of magnitude).

The student compares findings with two peer-reviewed studies (Gorrisen 2018; Hou 2019) and protein-content literature, explaining how the results align with existing knowledge.  The discussion is relevant and integrated, fulfilling the requirement for justified comparison, though depth could be extended to mechanistic detailâhence 5.

The student situates the research question firmly within a concrete, relevant context: vegan athletes in the school who need plant-based BCAA sources.  Background subsections (1.1â1.6) articulate nutritional motivation, prior studies and hypothesised rankings, demonstrating a specific rather than merely broad framing.  The context is appropriate, detailed and directly linked to the articulated question, satisfying the descriptor for the highest band.

Instrument uncertainties are listed and percentage uncertainties calculated, displaying awareness.  Nevertheless, propagation to means or the ANOVA outcome is absent, and one percentage computation is incorrect (micropipette 0.20 ÂµL/10 ÂµL = 2 %, not 0.2 %).  Such significant omissions place the work in the mid band.

For every stated weakness, a realistic and relevant improvement is proposed with an accompanying explanation of the expected effect on data quality.  Suggestions (fixed LED lighting, digital callipers, larger TLC plates) are feasible and directly tackle the identified limitations.  Clarity and linkage warrant a high-band score, slightly reduced for absence of cost/feasibility discussion.

Methodological issues (independent/controlled variables, confounding variable, safety, equipment uncertainties) are not merely listed; rationale is provided for each control and for solvent choice, detection method and statistical test.  However, some explanations remain surface-level (e.g. choice of acetoneâbutanol system is asserted rather than fully justified against alternatives), so an excellent yet not perfect explanation is evidenced.  Hence a high score within the top band is warranted.

Step-by-step procedures (sample preparation, TLC set-up, data collection, reading) with quantities, timings and equipment allow another investigator to replicate the work.  Minor ambiguities (composition of âethanolâbutanolâ mixture, unclear camera settings) mean reproduction would still be possible but may require small assumptions, so a score of 5 (rather than 6) is most representative.

annotations.comment: In the alternative hypothesis, the wording âprotein contents per gramâ may be ambiguous. Specify whether this refers to mass/weight percent or concentration to avoid confusion.
Table 1 clearly lists protein contents and hypothesised ranks, but column headings lack units for mass. Add â(g per g sample)â to reinforce clarity.
Controlled variables table is detailed with rationale and control methods, enabling reproducibility with minimal ambiguity.
The micropipette drop volume is assumed as 1 ÂµL per drop without validation. Calibrate drop volume or use direct pipetting to ensure precise delivery.
Cost and feasibility of improvements (e.g., digital callipers, LED setup) are not discussed. Include brief feasibility assessment to complete evaluation.
Table 11 is empty, lacking listed causes and improvements for random errors. Populate this table to complete evaluation of random error sources.
The research question is framed within a specific and relevant context (vegan athletesâ dietary needs), demonstrating excellent context setting for the investigation.
The Â±0.2 ÂµL uncertainty for the 10 ÂµL sample is included but its origin isnât justified. Clarify how this instrument precision was determined (e.g., calibration data).
The background justifies selecting food samples via a survey yet omits survey design details. Include sampling method, respondent numbers and questions to enhance reproducibility.
The chemical description of BCAAs is clear and precise, with appropriate molecular detail and structures, supporting the investigationâs foundational context.
The rationale for choosing acetone as mobile phase lacks comparison with other solvents. Discuss alternative systems (e.g., butanolâmethanol) to justify selection.
The function of ninhydrin is accurately described with chemical logic, enhancing understanding of qualitative detection of amino acids.
The literature review on HSL integration is well linked to colour quantification, but it would benefit from citing validation studies demonstrating HSL accuracy in similar analyses.
Substances list specifies concentrations but omits preparation methods for the ethanolâbutanol mixture. Include precise solvent ratios and mixing protocol.
TLC plate preparation steps are clear, but environmental conditions (humidity, temperature) during plate cutting are unaddressed. Control or note them for consistency.
Data collection procedure is systematically described, but camera distance and angle during photo capture must be fixed or documented to ensure consistent lightness readings.
Safety, ethical and environmental considerations are thoughtfully addressed with appropriate PPE and waste protocols, demonstrating thorough methodological consideration.
Qualitative data table lists spot colours and intensity but lacks a defined scale or measurement criteria for âhighâ vs âlowââdefine these to improve precision.
Raw data table is comprehensive and well-organized, with trials clearly labelled for each amino acid and food sample.
The percentage uncertainty for the micropipette is calculated as 0.2% using 0.02/10.0, but the numerator should be 0.20 ÂµL, giving 2%. Correct this significant error.
Uncertainties are individually calculated but not propagated into mean values or ANOVA. Include error propagation to fully consider uncertainties.
The standard deviation calculation is correctly executed, but the reliability rule (SD <33% mean) is a weak measure. Consider using coefficient of variation.
Table 7 omits P. americanaâs reliability assessment, yet it was tested. Include all samples in the summary table for completeness and consistency.
Graph 1 effectively visualizes mean lightness values for comparison, with clear axes labels and error bars.
The ANOVA section lacks mention of normality and homogeneity checks. Report tests or graphical analyses to justify ANOVA assumptions.
The conclusion directly addresses the research question and is fully consistent with the presented analysis, correctly interpreting inverse L values as concentration measures.
The P-value is reported as 0 rather than indicating â<â0.001â or similar. Provide appropriate significant figures and context for statistical reporting.
The conclusion compares findings to the hypothesis and peer-reviewed studies, justifying alignment with existing literature.
The mechanistic basis for the positive correlation between protein and BCAA is asserted rather than explained. Discuss biochemical pathways to deepen scientific context.
Outliers in P. vulgaris and P. americana SD are identified, demonstrating strong evaluation of data reliability.
The discussion of uncertainty impact is brief and doesnât quantify how the 9.3% total uncertainty affects conclusions. Explain its relative influence on reliability.
Evaluation strengths are clearly described, highlighting methodological merits and reliable data clustering.
The proposed methodological improvements are relevant and realistic, directly addressing identified systematic and random errors.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Improvements
